title: Contributing to okwaayeli
text: Thank interest contributing. okwaayeli research package decomposes Ghanaian crop-production shortfalls farmer technical inefficiency ecological / socio-economic technology gaps. Contributions welcome researchers, graduate students, methodologists, documentation editors alike. document covers: Code conduct Repository layout — put things set working environment standard study pipeline (001 → 100) Adding new study Adding modifying harmonized dataset Coding style conventions Documentation conventions Submitting pull request Reporting bugs suggesting features

title: Contributing to okwaayeli
text: Participation project governed Contributor Covenant. participating, agree abide terms. Report unacceptable behaviour ftsiboe@hotmail.com.

title: Contributing to okwaayeli
text: put things:
code: okwaayeli/ ├── R/                              # Package source — the analytical engine ├── man/                            # Auto-generated function documentation ├── data-raw/                       # Sources used to build the harmonized data │   └── releases/harmonized_data/   # 11 published .dta files + codebook ├── replications/                   # One folder per study │   └── <study_name>/ │       ├── 001_DATA_<study>.R │       ├── 002_MATCHING_<study>.R │       ├── 003_TREATMENT_<study>.R │       ├── 004_MSF_<study>.R │       ├── 100_FIGTAB_<study>.R │       ├── 005_Release_<study>.R   # optional, prepares the public bundle │       ├── job_msf.sbatch          # optional, SLURM job script │       ├── <study>.Rmd / README.md │       └── output/ ├── manuscripts/                    # Manuscript drafts, exhibits, literature ├── tests/                          # testthat harness ├── vignettes/                      # Long-form documentation └── .github/                        # CI, issue templates, PR template

title: Contributing to okwaayeli
text: need: R ≥ 4.1 Stata installation (data harmonization data-raw/okwaayeli_DATA.Stata; rest pipeline R) system libraries required MatchIt, frontier, micEcon, rgenoud, quadprog, optmatch, dbarts, CBPS. Debian/Ubuntu usual build-essential, libgmp-dev, libssl-dev, libxml2-dev set suffices. Install development version: , development clone: Harmonized data bundled package. downloaded first call get_household_data() hh_data GitHub release: Authentication uses GHProdLab_TOKEN environment variable set, otherwise standard gh CLI / git credential helpers.
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("ftsiboe/okwaayeli") devtools::load_all() devtools::document() devtools::test() devtools::check() farmer_data <- okwaayeli::get_household_data("harmonized_crop_farmer_data")

title: Contributing to okwaayeli
text: Every active study follows five-stage pipeline. stage numbered R script. Output written replications/<study>/output/. SLURM users: replication folder ships job_msf.sbatch array execution. run_only_for() helper R/helpers.R lets target specific subset draw IDs.
code: 001_DATA      Pull the relevant harmonized .dta files, restrict the sample,               build the treatment indicator(s) and the covariate set.  002_MATCHING  For every matching specification declared by               write_match_formulas(), build matched samples via               match_sample_specifications() and write covariate balance               statistics with covariate_balance().  003_TREATMENT Compute per-spec average treatment effects on the treated               (and other heterogeneity dimensions) via               treatment_effect_calculation() / treatment_effect_summary().  004_MSF       Run the meta-stochastic-frontier estimation across draws               (see draw_msf_estimations() and draw_msf_summary()). Outputs               go to output/estimations/ and output/te_summary.rds.  100_FIGTAB    Render the final figures and tables (.R + .do siblings).               Writes the canonical *_results.xlsx exhibit.  005_Release   (optional) Package the public replication bundle.

title: Contributing to okwaayeli
text: cleanest way bootstrap new study copy replications/disability/ template, : Rename scripts *_disability_study.R *_<your_study>_study.R. Edit 001_DATA_<your_study>_study.R pull harmonized datasets need (use get_household_data("harmonized_<…>_data")). Edit 002_MATCHING_<your_study>_study.R declare treatment variable variables want balance . Add replications/<your_study>/<your_study>.Rmd describing study, contributors, (eventually) abstract. Open Study proposal issue appears lab roadmap reviewers can flag duplication risks. Submit PR adding study folder row main README’s “Work progress” / “Scheduled studies” list. study needs new harmonized dataset, see §6.

what: 6. Adding or modifying a harmonized dataset
title: Contributing to okwaayeli
text: harmonized datasets live data-raw/releases/harmonized_data/ published hh_data GitHub release. Updating one multi-step process: Modify Stata harmonization data-raw/okwaayeli_DATA.(per-topic block within ). Run harmonization end--end put new .dta file data-raw/releases/harmonized_data/. Update data-raw/releases/harmonized_data/codebook.md new / changed variable definitions. Run data-raw/scripts/001_harmonized_data_release.R push new release asset via piggyback. Bump Version: field DESCRIPTION add NEWS.md entry describing data change. Existing studies consume dataset re-run *_results.xlsx exhibits regenerated separate commit.

title: Contributing to okwaayeli
text: Line length: aim ≤ 100 columns. Naming: snake_case functions data, lowerCamelCase short loop / index variables readability requires . Pipes: native R |> preferred %>%; magrittr dependency. Comments: prefer roxygen comments every exported function (@param, @return, @examples, @export). exported functions must least one runnable @examples block (use \dontrun{} requires external data). Globals: data.table / dplyr free-variable warnings suppressed via utils::globalVariables() R/zzz.R, adding .data$ pronouns inline. Random seeds: every stochastic operation reproducible. Use okwaayeli_control()$myseed (currently 11122025) pass set.seed() top draw_* call. library() calls R/. Use pkg::fn() import via roxygen @importFrom.

title: Contributing to okwaayeli
text: Function docs: every exported function carries roxygen block. Run devtools::document() committing. READMEs: study-level README.md files generated *.Rmd. Edit .Rmd, render rmarkdown::render("…/README.Rmd", output_format = "github_document", output_file = "README.md"). Vignettes: long-form -tos go vignettes/. New vignettes need \VignetteIndexEntry{} build devtools::check(). NEWS.md: every PR touching R/, data-raw/, public function must add entry appropriate version heading.

title: Contributing to okwaayeli
text: Fork repository branch main write access. Use descriptive branch name: feature/<short-name>, fix/<short-name>, docs/<short-name>, study/<study-name>. Make focused commits — one logical change per commit. Prefer imperative-mood, ≤ 72-char subject lines (e.g. Add land-tenure MSF spec). Run devtools::check() locally. PRs gated R-CMD-check GitHub Actions; failing CI blocks merge. changed exported functions examples, run devtools::document() commit regenerated man/ files PR. Open PR, fill template, request review @ftsiboe (relevant CODEOWNERS).

title: Contributing to okwaayeli
text: Use issue templates — one bug reports, one new-study proposals, one data requests. methodology questions open-ended discussions, please use GitHub Discussions rather opening issue. Sensitive security reports follow SECURITY.md. Constructive feedback appreciated. Thank helping make agricultural-productivity research Ghana reproducible.

title: Security policy
text: okwaayeli research package distributes harmonized survey data econometric tooling. handle authentication personally identifiable information beyond de-identified records released Ghana Statistical Service Ghana Living Standards Surveys. Nonetheless, take security code base consumers seriously.

title: Security policy
text: provide security fixes latest tagged release main. new version released, prior versions longer patched.

title: Security policy
text: believe found security vulnerability okwaayeli — example remote-code-execution risk one R/ functions, path-traversal issue study_setup(), credential leak data download path — please open public issue. Instead, send private report ftsiboe@hotmail.com : description vulnerability impact. Steps (minimal reproducer) demonstrate issue. suggested mitigation. can expect: acknowledgement within 5 business days. status update within 14 days, including either fix timeline decision report constitute security issue. Public disclosure (credit, wish) fix shipped.

title: Security policy
text: Please use channel : Statistical questions methodology — use GitHub Discussions. Bugs security impact — use bug report template. Data harmonization requests — use data request template.

title: Security policy
text: okwaayeli codebase deliberately avoids: Reading writing files outside user-specified study_setup() tree unless explicitly authorised. Storing credentials. optional GHProdLab_TOKEN environment variable read function call sites never written disk. Phoning home telemetry. spot deviation properties, please follow private-disclosure path .

title: Meta-stochastic frontiers and statistical matching: the okwaayeli pipeline
text: Sub-Saharan crop yields typically 30–60% global benchmarks. shortfall two distinct sources: Technical inefficiency — farmers operating best practice already available district. Technology gap — entire regions (sub-populations) lacking inputs, varieties, know-define global / national production frontier. two sources call different policy responses. Closing inefficiency mainly needs extension managerial support; closing technology gaps needs investment inputs, varieties, infrastructure. decide scarce policy attention go, need decompose observed output shortfall. okwaayeli implements decomposition meta-stochastic-frontier (MSF) framework (Huang, Huang, Liu, 2014) coupled statistical matching (Abadie & Imbens, 2006, 2016) group comparisons (e.g. farmers vs without disabilities) contaminated selection observables.

title: Meta-stochastic frontiers and statistical matching: the okwaayeli pipeline
text: study replications/<study>/ follows skeleton.

title: Meta-stochastic frontiers and statistical matching: the okwaayeli pipeline
text: study_setup() creates standard folder tree replications/<project_name>/output/ returns resolved paths plus reproducible seed (default 11122025, set via okwaayeli_control()).
code: library(okwaayeli)  study_environment <- study_setup(project_name = "tutorial")

title: Meta-stochastic frontiers and statistical matching: the okwaayeli pipeline
text: get_household_data() downloads requested .dta file hh_data GitHub release via piggyback, caches tools::R_user_dir("okwaayeli", "cache"), returns data.frame. Typical 001 work merge: restrict survey waves, build treatment indicator (e.g. disabled <- .integer(disabled_self == 1 | …)), build covariate list.
code: farmer_data     <- get_household_data("harmonized_crop_farmer_data") disability_data <- get_household_data("harmonized_disability_data")  study_data <- merge(   farmer_data, disability_data,   by = c("Surveyx", "EaId", "HhId", "Mid") )

title: Meta-stochastic frontiers and statistical matching: the okwaayeli pipeline
text: matching engine evaluates several specifications (nearest-neighbour, CBPS, propensity-score, BART, etc.) parallel reports standardised mean differences can pick best-balanced one. Output written output/matching/.
code: formulas <- write_match_formulas(   treatment = "disabled",   covariates = c("Female", "Age", "YerEdu", "HouseholdSize", "Region") )  matched <- match_sample_specifications(   data     = study_data,   formulas = formulas,   weight   = "Weight" )  balance <- covariate_balance(matched)

title: Meta-stochastic frontiers and statistical matching: the okwaayeli pipeline
text: Outputs land output/treatment_effects/ feed MSF estimation final exhibits.
code: draws <- draw_matched_samples(matched, n_draws = 100)  te_summary <- treatment_effect_calculation(   draws  = draws,   data   = study_data,   outcomes = c("HrvstKg", "FertKg", "SeedKg") )  treatment_effect_summary(te_summary)

title: Meta-stochastic frontiers and statistical matching: the okwaayeli pipeline
text: MSF block returns input elasticities, returns scale, technical efficiency (TE), technology-gap ratios (TGR), meta-frontier performance metric (TE × TGR). Outputs land output/estimations/. real studies typically run draws 1..N parallel via SLURM job_msf.sbatch script ships replication folder, aggregate.
code: msf_specs <- list(   output_variable  = "HrvstKg",   input_variables  = c("Area", "LabHr", "FertKg", "SeedKg", "PstcdHr"),   inefficiency_covariates = list(     Svarlist = c("YerEdu", "Female", "Region"),     Fvarlist = c("Surveyx")   ),   risk_covariates  = list(Svarlist = c("Region")),   weight_variable  = "Weight" )  est <- draw_msf_estimations(   draw                 = 0,   drawlist             = draws,   data                 = study_data,   output_variable      = msf_specs$output_variable,   input_variables      = msf_specs$input_variables,   inefficiency_covariates = msf_specs$inefficiency_covariates,   risk_covariates      = msf_specs$risk_covariates,   weight_variable      = msf_specs$weight_variable )  summary_est <- draw_msf_summary(est, technology_legend = c("With", "Without"))

title: Meta-stochastic frontiers and statistical matching: the okwaayeli pipeline
text: final stage writes canonical *_results.xlsx exhibit plus publication-ready figures output/figure/ output/figure_data/. shell script study-specific exhibits vary, inputs always per-draw estimation .rds files written step 4.

title: Meta-stochastic frontiers and statistical matching: the okwaayeli pipeline
text: ?study_setup ?okwaayeli_control project scaffolding. ?get_household_data data download mechanics. ?msf_workhorse ?sf_workhorse core estimators. disability study replications/disability/ fully worked example.

title: Meta-stochastic frontiers and statistical matching: the okwaayeli pipeline
text: Huang, C. J., Huang, T. H., & Liu, N. H. (2014). new approach estimating metafrontier production function based stochastic frontier framework. Journal Productivity Analysis, 42(3), 241–254. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11123-014-0402-2 Abadie, ., & Imbens, G. W. (2006). Large sample properties matching estimators average treatment effects. Econometrica, 74(1), 235–267. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1468-0262.2006.00655.x Abadie, ., & Imbens, G. W. (2016). Matching estimated propensity score. Econometrica, 84(2), 781–807. https://doi.org/10.3982/ECTA11293

title: okwaayeli — Agricultural Productivity in Ghana
text: primary objective project determine whether agricultural production shortfalls Ghana driven farmer technical inefficiency, technology gaps, combination . compiles series research articles assess farmer-specific institutional factors, exploring influence technical inefficiency adoption advanced agricultural technologies. identifying root causes production shortfalls, project provides empirical evidence crucial policy discussions aimed enhancing agricultural productivity contexts limited access new technologies.

title: okwaayeli — Agricultural Productivity in Ghana
text: project utilizes farm-level data Ghana Living Standards Surveys (GLSS), population-based surveys conducted periodically, approximately every five years, Ghana: GLSS1, GLSS2, GLSS3, GLSS4, GLSS5, GLSS6, GLSS7. harmonized data products documented data-raw/releases/harmonized_data/codebook.md distributed hh_data release.

title: okwaayeli — Agricultural Productivity in Ghana
text: Meta-Stochastic Frontier (MSF) model, introduced Huang, Huang, & Liu (2014), employed capture technological heterogeneity technical inefficiencies across farmers. minimize self-selection bias reduce model dependency, matching approach also utilized applicable (Abadie Imbens 2006; Abadie Imbens 2016). worked walkthrough full pipeline, see methodology vignette.

title: okwaayeli — Agricultural Productivity in Ghana
text: Every active study follows five-stage pipeline. stage numbered R script whose output feeds next.
code:
flowchart LR A[Harmonized GLSS data
get_household_data()] --> B[001_DATA
sample & covariates] B --> C[002_MATCHING
specs & balance] C --> D[003_TREATMENT
treatment effects] D --> E[004_MSF
frontier estimation] E --> F[100_FIGTAB
results.xlsx + figures]

what: Compute Jenks-Binned Shares by Aggregation Groups — compute_jenks_binned_shares
title: Compute Jenks-Binned Shares by Aggregation Groups — compute_jenks_binned_shares
text: function bins numeric variable using Jenks (Fisher) natural breaks computes relative share counts /weights within aggregation group across binned ranges. useful summarizing distribution variable (e.g., dealer density, yield, acreage, distances) across spatial categorical groups.

title: Compute Jenks-Binned Shares by Aggregation Groups — compute_jenks_binned_shares
code: compute_jenks_binned_shares(   dt,   output_variable,   aggregation_points,   jenks = NULL,   jenks_number = NULL,   weight_variable = NULL )

title: Compute Jenks-Binned Shares by Aggregation Groups — compute_jenks_binned_shares
text: dt data.table containing data. output_variable Character name numeric column bin. aggregation_points Character vector grouping variables (e.g., "region", "district", "grid_id"). jenks Optional numeric vector breakpoints. NULL, Jenks breaks computed automatically. jenks_number Integer number Jenks classes compute jenks NULL. weight_variable Optional character name weighting column. NULL, weight 1 assumed row.

title: Compute Jenks-Binned Shares by Aggregation Groups — compute_jenks_binned_shares
text: data.table : grouping variables binned_range_name - character label Jenks bin binned_range_level - numeric factor level bin estimate_count - share counts within group estimate_weight - share weights within group

title: Compute Jenks-Binned Shares by Aggregation Groups — compute_jenks_binned_shares
text: Internally function: Computes Jenks natural breaks (provided) Creates count weight totals within bin group Merges total counts/weights per group Computes shares

what: Compute Covariate Balance Summaries Across Matching Specs — covariate_balance
title: Compute Covariate Balance Summaries Across Matching Specs — covariate_balance
text: Compute Covariate Balance Summaries Across Matching Specs

title: Compute Covariate Balance Summaries Across Matching Specs — covariate_balance
code: covariate_balance(match_specifications, matching_output_directory)

title: Compute Covariate Balance Summaries Across Matching Specs — covariate_balance
text: match_specifications data.frame/data.table least columns: boot, ARRAY, spec fields stored RDS (e.g., method, distance, link). matching_output_directory Directory containing RDS files named "0001.rds", "0002.rds", etc.

title: Compute Covariate Balance Summaries Across Matching Specs — covariate_balance
text: list : rate data.frame mean balance metrics spec (Adj sample ) composite rate. bal_tab long-format balance table per covariate/stat/sample/spec.

title: Compute Covariate Balance Summaries Across Matching Specs — covariate_balance
text: Reads matching result RDS (expected contain m.match_specifications), extracts balance via cobalt::bal.tab, reshapes, computes composite balance \(rate = mean( (Diff-0)^2, (KS-0)^2, (V_Ratio-1)^2 )\), averages ARRAY, method, distance, link, sample.

what: Draw matched samples (stratified bootstrap + matching) — draw_matched_samples
title: Draw matched samples (stratified bootstrap + matching) — draw_matched_samples
text: Performs stratified resampling Surveyx, EaId level (excluding EaIds listed current bootstrap ID) computes adjusted sampling weights used matching. fits matching model per match_specifications[, ] using MatchIt, exact matching Emch distance/link match_specifications.

title: Draw matched samples (stratified bootstrap + matching) — draw_matched_samples
code: draw_matched_samples(   i,   data,   match_variables_exact,   match_variables_scaler,   match_variables_factor,   match_specifications,   sample_draw_list,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Draw matched samples (stratified bootstrap + matching) — draw_matched_samples
text: Integer index selecting row match_specifications use. data data.frame/data.table containing least: Surveyx, EaId, HhId, Mid, unique_identifier, Weight, Treat, plus columns named Emch, Scle, Fixd. match_variables_exact character vector variable names included exact match component (e.g., "gender", "region"). match_variables_scaler character vector continuous scalar variable names include covariates general match formula (e.g., "age", "income"). match_variables_factor character vector factor variable names included dummy-coded categorical covariates general match formula. match_specifications Data frame matching specifications columns boot, method, distance, optionally link. sample_draw_list Data frame column ID identifies bootstrap draw, remaining column corresponds Survey containing sampled EaId. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints chosen matching method timing. Default FALSE.

title: Draw matched samples (stratified bootstrap + matching) — draw_matched_samples
text: list : match_specifications selected row match_specifications. m.matchit object. md Matched data: Surveyx, EaId, HhId, Mid, unique_identifier, weights, pWeight. df analysis data adjusted weights: Surveyx, EaId, HhId, Mid, unique_identifier, pWeight.

title: Draw matched samples (stratified bootstrap + matching) — draw_matched_samples
text: Adjusted weights computed \(pWeight = Weight \times (alloc/allocj)\), alloc pre-exclusion sum Weight Surveyx, EaId allocj post-exclusion sum. Exact matching performed variables Emch. distance model formula constructed Treat ~ Scle + Fixd. distance == "glm", m.order = "largest" used; otherwise "closest".

what: Run a single MSF draw and summarize stochastic frontier results — draw_msf_estimations
title: Run a single MSF draw and summarize stochastic frontier results — draw_msf_estimations
text: Performs one iteration multi-stage stochastic frontier (MSF) estimation given draw. function () filters analysis sample using draw-specific exclusion list, (ii) calls msf_workhorse() estimate production, inefficiency, risk components survey, (iii) optionally builds disaggregated efficiency score summaries.

title: Run a single MSF draw and summarize stochastic frontier results — draw_msf_estimations
code: draw_msf_estimations(   draw,   drawlist,   surveyy = FALSE,   data,   output_variable,   input_variables,   inefficiency_covariates = NULL,   risk_covariates = NULL,   weight_variable = NULL,   production_slope_shifters = NULL,   intercept_shifters = NULL,   f,   d,   identifiers,   include_trend = FALSE,   technology_variable = NULL,   matching_type = NULL,   adoption_covariates = NULL,   intercept_shifters_meta = NULL,   disagscors_list = NULL,   match_specifications,   match_specification_optimal,   match_path )

title: Run a single MSF draw and summarize stochastic frontier results — draw_msf_estimations
text: draw Integer (numeric coercible integer). Draw iteration index. Typically 0 used full baseline sample positive integers resampled matched draws. drawlist data.frame similar object containing exclusion information draw. Rows correspond draws, column ID identifying draw subsequent columns containing EaId values remove data draw. surveyy Logical; TRUE, uses survey labels stored data$Surveyx. FALSE (default), observations assigned synthetic survey labeled "GLSS0" estimation. data data.frame data.table containing estimation sample. Must include, minimum, columns referenced arguments (e.g., output_variable, input_variables, identifiers, weight_variable, technology matching variables, variables used disagscors_list). output_variable Character scalar. Name dependent (output) variable used production frontier. input_variables Character vector input (production) variables entering stochastic frontier. inefficiency_covariates Optional named list specifying variables inefficiency function. Typical structure: list(Svarlist = c(...), Fvarlist = c(...)). risk_covariates Optional named list specifying variables production risk (noise) function. NULL, homoskedastic noise term usually implied. weight_variable Optional character scalar giving name sampling weight variable data. Used computing weighted summaries (e.g., weighted means) efficiency scores. production_slope_shifters Character scalar naming variable shifts production-function slope (e.g., technology shifter). Defaults NULL indicate slope shifter. intercept_shifters Optional named list intercept shifter variables baseline (unmatched) sample. Typical structure: list(Svarlist = c(...), Fvarlist = c(...)). f Functional form identifier passed msf_workhorse() (e.g., Cobb-Douglas, translog). exact meaning handled workhorse function. d Distributional form identifier inefficiency term (e.g., half-normal, truncated-normal), passed msf_workhorse(). identifiers Character vector variable names uniquely identify units (e.g., household, plot, observation IDs). used merge efficiency scores back data disaggregated summaries. include_trend Logical; TRUE, includes technology trend variable (given technology_variable) frontier. Defaults FALSE. technology_variable Optional character scalar naming technology (trend) variable used include_trend = TRUE. matching_type Optional variable object controlling nearest-neighbor matching; passed msf_workhorse(). expected type structure depend implementation workhorse function. adoption_covariates Optional named list specifying inefficiency-function variables matched sample (post-matching specification). structure inefficiency_covariates. intercept_shifters_meta Optional named list intercept shifters matched sample. structure intercept_shifters. disagscors_list Optional character vector variable names disaggregated efficiency score summaries computed. variable list, function builds weighted unweighted summaries TE/TE0/TGR/MTE survey, sample, disaggregation level. match_specifications match specifications match_specification_optimal match specifications match_path match path

title: Run a single MSF draw and summarize stochastic frontier results — draw_msf_estimations
text: named list components: sf_estm - Combined frontier parameter estimates across surveys (rows tagged Surveyy draw). el_mean, ef_mean, rk_mean - Mean/summary statistics elasticities, efficiency, risk metrics. ef_dist, rk_dist - Distributions efficiency risk quantities. el_samp, ef_samp, rk_samp - Sample-level results (retained draw == 0). disagscors - Optional disaggregated efficiency summary table disagscors_list NULL; otherwise NULL. error occurs anywhere pipeline, function returns NULL.

title: Run a single MSF draw and summarize stochastic frontier results — draw_msf_estimations
text: main outputs include model estimates, mean/percentile summaries, (optionally) disaggregated efficiency statistics user-specified grouping variables. draws zero, sample-level results dropped reduce storage. Draw-specific filtering: Using drawlist, function removes EaId values associated current draw data. allows bootstrap, jackknife, matched-sample resampling. Survey-specific estimation: setting Surveyy label (either Surveyx collapsed "GLSS0"), function loops unique survey labels calls msf_workhorse() . workhorse expected return list components sf_estm, el_mean, ef_mean, rk_mean, ef_dist, rk_dist, el_samp, ef_samp, rk_samp. Disaggregated efficiency summaries: disagscors_list provided, function constructs weighted unweighted efficiency statistics (e.g., weighted mean, mean, median, mode) TE/TE0/TGR/MTE survey, sample, restriction status, technology, disaggregation level. Results stored res$disagscors. Draw-specific pruning: draw != 0, sample-level objects (el_samp, ef_samp, rk_samp) removed returned list reduce memory usage, keeping aggregate results. Internally, function uses data.table, dplyr, tidyr, doBy, crayon data manipulation progress messages. estimation delegated msf_workhorse().

title: Summarize multi-draw Meta Stochastic Frontier (MSF) results — draw_msf_summary

title: Summarize multi-draw Meta Stochastic Frontier (MSF) results — draw_msf_summary
code: draw_msf_summary(res, technology_legend)

title: Summarize multi-draw Meta Stochastic Frontier (MSF) results — draw_msf_summary
text: res List draw-level result objects. element expected list (least) following components: sf_estm: coefficient-level estimates diagnostics naive, group, meta-frontiers, including draw index. ef_mean: aggregated efficiency statistics (e.g., TE0, TE, TGR, MTE) sample, technology, survey, estimation type. el_mean: aggregated elasticity statistics input, technology, survey. rk_mean: aggregated risk statistics, available. ef_dist: histogram-style efficiency distributions (counts/weights) value ranges (binned_range_name, binned_range_level). rk_dist: histogram-style risk distributions, available. disagscors: optional disaggregated efficiency summaries subgroup levels. el_samp, ef_samp, rk_samp: observation-level elasticities, efficiencies, risk measures least baseline draw (usually draw == 0). component must include draw column distinguishes baseline draw (typically draw == 0) resampled draws (draw != 0). technology_legend Data.frame providing mapping numeric technology codes labels, typically least: Tech: numeric technology index used internally MSF estimation, corresponding label column (e.g., original technology_variable). used compute technology gaps (e.g., efficiency disaggregated efficiency gaps) relative reference technology (smallest value   technology_legend$Tech).

title: Summarize multi-draw Meta Stochastic Frontier (MSF) results — draw_msf_summary
text: list following components: sf_estm: Data.frame jackknife summary statistics frontier coefficients diagnostics across draws, columns Estimate, Estimate.mean, Estimate.sd, Estimate.length, jack_zv, jack_pv, indexed Survey, CoefName, Tech, sample, restrict. ef_mean: Jackknife summaries efficiency statistics (TE0, TE, TGR, MTE), sample, technology, survey, type, estimation type, statistic, restriction. el_mean: Jackknife summaries elasticity statistics, input, technology, survey, statistic, restriction. rk_mean: (available) jackknife summaries risk statistics, type = "risk". ef_dist: Jackknife summaries efficiency distributions (counts weights) ranges, stat indicating whether row refers counts weights. rk_dist: (available) jackknife summaries risk distributions ranges, type = "risk". disagscors: (available) jackknife summaries disaggregated efficiency statistics gaps subgroup level technology. el_samp: Observation-level elasticity outputs first draw res (typically baseline draw). ef_samp: Observation-level efficiency scores first draw res. rk_samp: Observation-level risk measures first draw res.

title: Summarize multi-draw Meta Stochastic Frontier (MSF) results — draw_msf_summary
text: function assumes element res output single draw MSF pipeline (e.g., msf_workhorse() followed draw_msf_estimations()), containing components : sf_estm, ef_mean, el_mean, rk_mean, ef_dist, rk_dist, (optionally) disagscors. component, function: Stacks draws using data.table::rbindlist(). Drops non-finite values (NA, Inf, -Inf, NaN). statistic (e.g., coefficient estimate, mean efficiency), identifies baseline draw (draw == 0) joins resampled draws (draw != 0) aggregated via doBy::summaryBy() compute: Estimate.mean: mean across non-baseline draws, Estimate.sd: standard deviation across non-baseline draws, Estimate.length: number non-baseline draws. Computes jackknife-style test statistic p-value: jack_zv = Estimate / Estimate.sd, jack_pv = 2 * (1 - pt(|jack_zv|, df = Estimate.length)), Estimate baseline draw statistic distribution resampled draws treated reference distribution. disaggregated scores (disagscors), function also: Computes technology-specific disaggregated efficiency gaps (level percent) relative reference technology   technology_legend, Aggregates gaps across draws jackknife fashion main efficiency statistics.

title: Meta stochastic frontier (MSF) workhorse — msf_workhorse
text: Performs Meta Stochastic Frontier (MSF) analysis several stages: Naive SF: Fits pooled stochastic frontier computes baseline technical efficiency (TE) measures. Group SF: technology variable technology_variable supplied, splits sample technology groups fits group-specific SF models. Meta SF (unmatched matched): Constructs meta-frontier using fitted values group models , optionally, nearest-neighbor matching specifications. , derives technology gap ratios (TGR) meta-technical efficiency (MTE). Summaries distributions: Produces weighted unweighted summaries distributional statistics efficiency, elasticity, risk across technologies, samples (unmatched/matched), surveys.

title: Meta stochastic frontier (MSF) workhorse — msf_workhorse
code: msf_workhorse(   data,   output_variable,   input_variables,   inefficiency_covariates = NULL,   risk_covariates = NULL,   weight_variable = NULL,   production_slope_shifters = NULL,   intercept_shifters = NULL,   f,   d,   identifiers,   include_trend = FALSE,   technology_variable = NULL,   matching_type = NULL,   adoption_covariates = NULL,   intercept_shifters_meta = NULL,   match_path,   match_specifications,   match_specification_optimal )

title: Meta stochastic frontier (MSF) workhorse — msf_workhorse
text: data data.frame data.table containing estimation sample. Must include dependent variable output_variable, inputs input_variables, weight variable weight_variable, unique ID variables identifiers, inefficiency risk covariates inefficiency_covariates risk_covariates, technology variable technology_variable (used), variables required matching objects disk (e.g., "Surveyx", "EaId", "HhId", "Mid", "unique_identifier"). output_variable Character scalar. Name dependent (output) variable used production frontier. input_variables Character vector input variable names (e.g., land, labor, capital) passed sf_workhorse. inefficiency_covariates Optional named list specifying variables inefficiency function naive group frontiers. Typical structure: list(scalar_variables = c(...), factor_variables = c(...)). risk_covariates Optional named list specifying variables production risk (noise) function naive group frontiers. structure inefficiency_covariates. NULL, homoskedastic noise term usually implied. weight_variable Character scalar. Name sampling/observation weight variable data. Observations zero weight dropped. production_slope_shifters Character scalar giving name slope-shifter variable data (e.g., technology shifter, policy dummy). Defaults NULL slope shifter. intercept_shifters Optional named list intercept shifter variables naive group frontiers. Typical structure: list(scalar_variables = c(...), factor_variables = c(...)), passed sf_workhorse. f Functional form index production frontier (e.g., Cobb-Douglas, translog, quadratic). index passed sf_workhorse ultimately internal form-selection utilities (e.g., sf_functional_forms). d Distributional form index inefficiency term (e.g., half-normal, truncated-normal). Passed sf_workhorse. identifiers Character vector variable names uniquely identify observations (e.g., c("unique_identifier","Survey","CropID","HhId","EaId","Mid")). IDs used merging scores summarizing technology sample. include_trend Logical; TRUE, last element input_variables treated trend/technology variable production function. Passed sf_workhorse. Defaults FALSE. technology_variable Optional character scalar naming technology variable (e.g., region, system, period) used define groups group SF meta-frontier estimation. NULL, technology groups formed naive frontier TE computed. matching_type Optional character scalar controlling nearest-neighbor matching specifications use meta-frontier estimation. NULL, function reads matching specifications matched samples "results/matching/" related RDS files. Setting matching_type = "optimal" restricts attention subset matching specifications labeled optimal. adoption_covariates Optional named list specifying inefficiency-function covariates meta-frontier (matched/unmatched) estimation. NULL, defaults inefficiency_covariates. intercept_shifters_meta Optional named list intercept shifters meta-frontier estimation. NULL, defaults intercept_shifters. match_path match path match_specifications match specifications match_specification_optimal match specifications

title: Meta stochastic frontier (MSF) workhorse — msf_workhorse
text: named list following components: sf_estm: Data.frame coefficient-level summaries naive, group, meta-frontiers (including LR tests), augmented technology identifiers (Tech) sample labels (e.g., "unmatched", matching-spec names). ef_mean: Data.frame aggregated efficiency statistics (TE0, TE, TGR, MTE), including weighted/unweighted means, medians, modes, survey, sample, technology, type, estimation type, restriction status. technology_variable supplied, also includes efficiency gap levels percentages. ef_dist: Data.frame efficiency distributions (histogram counts weights) efficiency ranges, survey, sample, technology, type, estimation type, restriction. rk_dist: Data.frame risk distributions (histogram counts weights) analogous ef_dist, type = "risk". el_mean: Data.frame aggregated elasticity statistics (weighted/unweighted means, medians, modes) input, survey, sample, technology, restriction, including elasticity-gap measures technology_variable provided. rk_mean: Data.frame aggregated risk statistics (weighted unweighted) survey, sample, technology, restriction, risk-gap measures technology_variable provided. el_samp: Data.frame observation-level elasticities models samples (excluding synthetic "GLSS0" survey rows). ef_samp: Data.frame observation-level efficiency scores (TE0, TE, TGR, MTE) including IDs, weights, technologies, sample labels. rk_samp: Data.frame observation-level risk measures models samples (excluding synthetic "GLSS0" survey rows).

title: Meta stochastic frontier (MSF) workhorse — msf_workhorse
text: function designed high-level orchestrator around sf_workhorse, assembling naive, group-level, meta-frontier results unified output structure suitable MSF analysis. workflow can summarized : Naive SFA (TE0): Calls sf_workhorse full sample obtain baseline efficiencies (teBC, teJLMS, teMO). technology variable specified (technology_variable = NULL), naive efficiencies final scores TE-related summaries produced. Group SFA (TE): technology_variable provided, sample recoded numeric technology groups (Tech), sf_workhorse applied separately group. Group-level efficiencies combined TE measures group survey. Meta-frontier (TGR MTE): Using fitted group-frontier values (Yhat) restricted models, function fits meta-frontiers (unmatched , optionally, matched) via sf_workhorse. Technology gap ratios (TGR) derived meta-frontiers, meta-technical efficiency (MTE) computed TE * TGR. Matching layer (optional): matching_type given, function loads matching specifications (mspecs, mspecs_optimal) corresponding matched samples disk (e.g., "results/matching/"), fits additional meta-frontiers matching specification, augments set scores summaries matched samples. Summaries distributions: Scores: TE0, TE, TGR, MTE reshaped long form aggregated compute weighted/unweighted means, medians, modes, distribution histograms (count-based weight-based) across surveys, samples, technologies, estimation types. technology_variable NULL, function also computes technology gaps (level percent) relative minimum technology   technology_legend. Elasticities: Elasticities underlying SF models (naive, group, meta) combined summarized similar way, including technology-gap metrics elasticities technology_variable provided. Risk: Risk measures derived sf_workhorse combined, summarized, used build distributional statistics analogous efficiency. LR tests: technology_variable NULL, function builds likelihood ratio test statistics comparing naive pooled frontier combination group meta-frontiers, storing rows CoefName = "LRT" sf_estm output. Throughout, function uses dplyr, tidyr, data.table, doBy, related helper functions reshaping summarizing output sf_workhorse.

Array ID restriction id = NULL, skip array-index filtering. numeric, match SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID. violation -> quit(save = "").

title: Stochastic frontier workhorse — sf_workhorse
text: Fits stochastic frontier model given production specification , monotonicity sufficiently violated, re-estimates shape-constrained (restricted) frontier using minimum-distance methods. function wraps underlying sfaR estimation routines set helper utilities (e.g., sf_functional_forms(), equation_editor(), fit_organizer(), translogEla(), curvature/monotonicity checks) produce: Unrestricted stochastic frontier estimates diagnostics. Shape-constrained estimates enforce monotonicity (related regularity conditions) needed. Observation-level efficiencies, fitted values, risk measures. Observation-level elasticities, including "returns--scale" term.

title: Stochastic frontier workhorse — sf_workhorse
code: sf_workhorse(   data,   output_variable,   input_variables,   weight_variable = NULL,   d,   f,   production_slope_shifters = NULL,   intercept_shifters = NULL,   inefficiency_covariates = NULL,   risk_covariates = NULL,   identifiers,   include_trend = FALSE )

title: Stochastic frontier workhorse — sf_workhorse
text: data data.frame data.table containing variables required estimation, including dependent variable output_variable, inputs input_variables, weight variable weight_variable (used), unique ID variables listed identifiers, variables referenced production_slope_shifters, intercept_shifters, inefficiency_covariates, risk_covariates. output_variable Character scalar. Name dependent/output variable data used production frontier. input_variables Character vector input variable names (e.g., land, labor, capital). order variables input_variables determines map generic input labels I1, I2, ... used functional-form utilities. weight_variable Optional character scalar. Name sampling/observation weight variable. NULL, observations given unit weight. d Distributional form index inefficiency term. passed sf_functional_forms() ultimately sfacross() (e.g., select half-normal, truncated-normal, etc.). exact mapping determined sf_functional_forms(). f Functional form index production frontier (e.g., Cobb-Douglas, translog, quadratic), used select list returned sf_functional_forms(). name chosen form (e.g., "CD", "TL", "QD", "LN") influences logging variables regularity checks. production_slope_shifters Character scalar giving name slope-shifter variable data (e.g., technology shift, policy dummy). Defaults NULL slope shifter passed equation_editor(). intercept_shifters Optional named list intercept shifter variables production function. Typical structure: list(scalar_variables = c(...), factor_variables = c(...)), specific interpretation handled inside equation_editor(). inefficiency_covariates Optional named list describing inefficiency-function covariates. Typical structure: list(scalar_variables = c(...), factor_variables = c(...)), passed uhet muhet sfacross() non-NULL. risk_covariates Optional named list describing production-risk (noise) covariates. non-NULL, used vhet sfacross() allow heteroskedasticity noise term. identifiers Character vector variable names uniquely identify observations (e.g., c("unique_identifier","Survey","CropID","HhId","EaId","Mid")). IDs carried efficiency, elasticity, risk outputs. include_trend Logical; TRUE, last element input_variables treated trend/technology variable handled accordingly functional-form elasticity calculations. Defaults FALSE.

title: Stochastic frontier workhorse — sf_workhorse
text: named list four data.frames: sf: Coefficient-level results combining unrestricted , applicable, restricted SFA estimates, plus monotonicity/curvature diagnostics. Columns include (minimum) CoefName, Estimate, StdError, Zvalue, Pvalue, restrict. ef: Observation-level efficiency results (unrestricted restricted), including ID variables identifiers, weights, efficiency measures (e.g., u), model-fitted values (mlFitted), restrict. el: Observation-level elasticities returns--scale-type measures. Includes IDs, weights, elasticity columns (e.g., el1, el2, ..., summed elasticity), restrict. rk: Observation-level risk measures (unrestricted restricted), including IDs, weights, risk, plus restrict.

title: Stochastic frontier workhorse — sf_workhorse
text: function proceeds several steps: Setup functional form selection: Using sf_functional_forms(), function determines appropriate production functional form distributional specification based number inputs (number_of_inputs), f d indices, include_trend flag. includes whether model specified logs (e.g., CD/TL/GP/TP) levels. Variable construction equation building: Inputs input_variables mapped generic labels (I1, I2, ...) log transforms created needed. outcome variable set Y lnY. function calls equation_editor() construct production, inefficiency, risk equations used sfacross(). Unrestricted stochastic frontier estimation: function iterates grid optimization methods (nr, nm, bfgs, bhhh, ...) tolerance settings, calling sfacross() successful convergence reported. extracts observation-level efficiencies via sfaR::efficiencies(), constructs fitted values, builds observation-level risk metric based squared deviations mean output. Elasticities regularity checks: Using fit_organizer() translogEla(), function computes elasticities returns--scale-type measures, evaluates monotonicity curvature via translogCheckMono() translogCheckCurvature() (coefficient-sign check CD/LN forms). Shape-constrained frontier (needed): monotonicity measure mono falls 0.80, function constructs set linear restrictions using translogMonoRestr() solves quadratic programming problem (via quadprog::solve.QP fall-back matrix inversions using Matrix, MASS, corpcor) obtain constrained coefficients. TL/QD forms constrained frontier (lcFitted) computed translogCalc(); CD/LN forms, constrained coefficients obtained via SEM representation using lavaan. Re-estimation constraints: Given constrained frontier, function rebuilds production equation equation_editor() re-estimates stochastic frontier (sfc) using optimization grid. Constrained efficiencies, risks, elasticities computed summarized, regularity checks repeated. Unrestricted restricted summaries stacked, restrict flag ("Unrestricted" vs. "Restricted"), downstream comparison.

text: Initialize Study Environment Directory Structure

title: Initialize Study Environment and Directory Structure — study_setup
code: study_setup(   myseed = 1980632,   project_name,   local_directories = list(home = file.path("replications", project_name), output =     file.path("replications", project_name, "output"), matching =     file.path("replications", project_name, "output", "matching"), treatment_effects =     file.path("replications", project_name, "output", "treatment_effects"), estimations =     file.path("replications", project_name, "output", "estimations"), figure_data =     file.path("replications", project_name, "output", "figure_data"), figure =     file.path("replications", project_name, "output", "figure")) )

title: Initialize Study Environment and Directory Structure — study_setup
text: myseed Numeric/integer scalar seed. coerced integer. Default: 1980632. project_name Length-1, non-NA character project name (required). local_directories Named list character paths create. Defaults use project_name.

title: Initialize Study Environment and Directory Structure — study_setup
text: List wd (directories) seed.

what: Compute log-linear treatment effects (ATE/ATET/ATEU) for multiple outcomes — treatment_effect_calculation
title: Compute log-linear treatment effects (ATE/ATET/ATEU) for multiple outcomes — treatment_effect_calculation
text: given matching specification index , function: Loads corresponding matched weights file matching_output_directory, Merges weights analysis data via unique_identifier, Optionally normalizes outcome first element outcome_variables (e.g., construct per-hectare per-unit measures), Fits weighted log-linear model outcome interactions Treat supplied matching covariate formulas, Transforms fitted differences percentage treatment effects, trims extreme values, computes weighted ATE, ATET, ATEU alongside model fit statistics.

title: Compute log-linear treatment effects (ATE/ATET/ATEU) for multiple outcomes — treatment_effect_calculation
code: treatment_effect_calculation(   data,   outcome_variables,   normalize = NULL,   i,   match_specifications,   matching_output_directory,   match_formulas )

title: Compute log-linear treatment effects (ATE/ATET/ATEU) for multiple outcomes — treatment_effect_calculation
text: data data.frame containing least: unique_identifier: unit identifier used merging matched weights, Treat: treatment indicator (logical 0/1), Area: plot size (positive; used input checks typical scaling), variables named outcome_variables, covariates referenced match_formulas$general_match match_formulas$exact_match. outcome_variables Character vector outcome variable names (e.g., c("HrvstKg","Area","SeedKg","HHLaborAE","HirdHr","FertKg","PestLt")). normalize Logical scalar. TRUE, outcome outcome_variables divided first element outcome_variables merged data (e.g., obtain outcomes per hectare per unit). Integer index selecting row match_specifications corresponding matching result file (zero-padded via ARRAY "NNNN.rds"). match_specifications data.frame least column ARRAY. -th row used locate matching output file cbind-ed returned results. matching_output_directory Directory containing per-specification matching results named "match_0001.rds", "match_0002.rds", etc. RDS expected contain element $md columns unique_identifier weights. match_formulas list : general_match: formula; RHS accessed via .character(...)[3], exact_match: formula; RHS accessed via .character(...)[2].

title: Compute log-linear treatment effects (ATE/ATET/ATEU) for multiple outcomes — treatment_effect_calculation
text: data.frame (atet_scalar) columns match_specifications[, ] repeated across rows, additional columns: outcome: outcome variable name, treatment: name treatment indicator (always "Treat"), level: one "ATE", "ATET", "ATEU", "aic", "ll", "R2", "N", "Ft", "R2a", est: numeric estimate corresponding level. One block rows returned per outcome outcome_variables. model fails given outcome, outcome skipped.

title: Compute log-linear treatment effects (ATE/ATET/ATEU) for multiple outcomes — treatment_effect_calculation
text: outcome oc outcome_variables, function: Filters observations non-missing Treat, outcome, weights, Constructs log-linear model form eps = min(oc[oc > 0]) * 1/100 estimation sample, Fits model using lm() weights given matched weights, Predicts fitted log outcomes treated (Treat = 1) untreated (Treat = 0) counterfactuals, Computes individual percentage treatment effects $$TE_OLS = \{ \exp(\hat{y}_1 - \hat{y}_0) - 1 \} \times 100,$$ trims interval \([-100, 100]\), forms: ATE: weighted mean TE_OLS units, ATET: weighted mean TE_OLS among treated units, ATEU: weighted mean TE_OLS among untreated units. addition, outcome function records model diagnostics: AIC, log-likelihood, \(R^2\), adjusted \(R^2\), F-statistic, sample size used regression.
code: log(oc + eps) ~ Treat * (general_match_rhs + exact_match_rhs)

what: Summarize Treatment Effect Estimates Across Matching Specifications — treatment_effect_summary
title: Summarize Treatment Effect Estimates Across Matching Specifications — treatment_effect_summary
text: Aggregates summarizes treatment effect results stored .rds files specified output directory. function reads RDS file, combines results single data frame, filters invalid estimates, computes jackknife-style summary statistics (mean, standard error, sample size, t-value, p-value) matching specification outcome.

title: Summarize Treatment Effect Estimates Across Matching Specifications — treatment_effect_summary
code: treatment_effect_summary(treatment_effects_output_directory)

title: Summarize Treatment Effect Estimates Across Matching Specifications — treatment_effect_summary
text: treatment_effects_output_directory Character string giving path directory containing .rds files treatment effect results (e.g., outputs treatment_effect_calculation() saved across specifications).

title: Summarize Treatment Effect Estimates Across Matching Specifications — treatment_effect_summary
text: data.frame containing summarized treatment effect estimates columns: method, distance, link, outcome, level est (original estimate non-bootstrap sample) jack_mean, jack_se, jack_n, jack_tvl, jack_pvl

title: Summarize Treatment Effect Estimates Across Matching Specifications — treatment_effect_summary
text: function proceeds following steps: Lists reads .rds files treatment_effects_output_directory. Combines single long-format data frame using data.table::rbindlist(). Removes rows invalid estimates (NA, NaN, Inf, -Inf). Separates base (non-bootstrap) results boot == 0 joins jackknife summary statistics computed via doBy::summaryBy() across method, distance, link, outcome, level. Renames summary columns : jack_mean: mean estimate jack_se: standard error jack_n: sample size (number replicates) jack_tvl: t-value (jack_mean / jack_se) jack_pvl: two-sided p-value

what: Construct Matching Formulas for Exact and General Matching — write_match_formulas
title: Construct Matching Formulas for Exact and General Matching — write_match_formulas
text: Builds two types matching formulas commonly used propensity score covariate matching workflows: exact match formula specifying categorical variables match exactly. general match formula specifying numeric factor covariates distance-based matching. function returns formulas character strings can used matching functions MatchIt::matchit() Matching::Match().

title: Construct Matching Formulas for Exact and General Matching — write_match_formulas
code: write_match_formulas(   match_variables_exact,   match_variables_scaler,   match_variables_factor )

title: Construct Matching Formulas for Exact and General Matching — write_match_formulas
text: match_variables_exact character vector variable names included exact match component (e.g., "gender", "region"). match_variables_scaler character vector continuous scalar variable names include covariates general match formula (e.g., "age", "income"). match_variables_factor character vector factor variable names included dummy-coded categorical covariates general match formula.

title: Construct Matching Formulas for Exact and General Matching — write_match_formulas
text: named list two elements: exact_match string representing exact match formula (factor variables ). general_match string representing general match formula, including continuous factor covariates right-hand side Treat ~.

title: okwaayeli (development version)
text: Added project-level pkgdown site config (_pkgdown.yml) GitHub Actions workflow publish GitHub Pages. Added methodology vignette (vignettes/msf--matching.Rmd) explaining meta-stochastic-frontier matching pipeline. Added harmonized-data codebook stub (data-raw/releases/harmonized_data/codebook.md) covering every released dataset. Greatly expanded CONTRIBUTING.md standard study pipeline (001_DATA → 100_FIGTAB), “add new study” instructions, coding/style conventions. Rewrote SECURITY.md real reporting process. Added inst/CITATION citation("okwaayeli") returns proper BibTeX. Added GitHub issue templates (bug, study proposal, data request), pull-request template, CODEOWNERS file. Added .gitattributes normalise line endings. Top-level README: added table contents, pipeline---glance diagram, citation block, corrected stale references legacy GHAgricProductivityLab repository name. Per-study replication READMEs: filled missing abstracts removed empty []() working-paper links.

title: okwaayeli 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial development version package (renamed GHAgricProductivityLab). Re-exports meta-stochastic-frontier statistical-matching engine used across legacy studies. See README canonical list published outputs.
